description: Premium Motor Spirit (PMS/Petrol) price per litre in Nigeria

sources.name: National Bureau of Statistics
sources.note: NBS publishes monthly 'Selected Food Prices' and 'Petroleum Products Prices' reports. Check e-Library for latest datasets.

sources.name: NNPC
sources.note: Official pricing authority since deregulation. NNPC Retail posts pump prices.

sources.name: PPPRA
sources.note: Legacy pricing regulator (Petroleum Products Pricing Regulatory Agency). Functions absorbed into NMDPRA (Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority) in 2021.

sources.name: BudgIT
sources.note: Civil society tracker. Publishes fuel subsidy analysis reports.

data_notes.methodology: Prices shown are official government-regulated or reference pump prices at time of announcement. Actual market prices may vary by location and supply conditions.
data_notes.verification: Each entry includes the policy event that triggered the price change. These are well-documented official gazette prices.
manual_update_steps: Visit NBS e-Library and download latest 'Petroleum Products Price Watch' report
Check NNPC Retail website for current depot and pump prices
Cross-reference with BudgIT subsidy tracker reports
For real-time prices, check major filling station chains (NNPC, Conoil, Total, etc.)

data.event: Price adjustment under Obasanjo administration
Incremental deregulation attempt
Price maintained through late Obasanjo / early Yar'Adua era
Regulated price held steady under Jonathan administration
Subsidy removal announcement by Jonathan government (Jan 1). Triggered Occupy Nigeria protests.
data.note: Price was briefly raised to 141 NGN, then partially rolled back to 97 NGN after nationwide protests.

data.event: Partial subsidy restoration after Occupy Nigeria protests. Compromise price of 97 NGN.
Price reduced under incoming Buhari administration as global oil prices fell
Price increase under Buhari government. Official regulated price set to 145 NGN.
Price reduced as global oil prices crashed during COVID-19 pandemic
Price increased to 162 NGN. Government declared move toward full deregulation.
Regulated price nominally at 165 NGN, though scarcity drove black-market prices higher in many areas.
President Tinubu announces fuel subsidy removal ('subsidy is gone') at inauguration on May 29. NNPC raises price. Lagos price circa 537 NGN.
Further price adjustment. Average national price around 568 NGN per NBS data.
Dangote Refinery not yet supplying PMS. Prices continue upward.
NNPC adjusts price amid naira devaluation and rising crude costs. Prices vary significantly by region (700-1000+ NGN).
data.note: Prices became increasingly deregulated with wide regional variation.

data.event: Dangote Refinery begins PMS supply. NNPC retail price around 998 NGN in some locations.
data.note: Prices varied: NNPC stations typically lower, independent marketers higher.

data.event: NBS October 2024 average retail price across Nigeria. 87.88% increase year-on-year.
NBS average retail PMS price. Peak pre-Dangote competitive pricing era.
Competitive pricing — Dangote refinery supply stabilized market. Significant reduction from January.
data.note: Prices dropped due to competition between Dangote Refinery and NNPC supply chains.

data.event: NBS January 2026 average retail price. 17.77% decrease year-on-year from Jan 2025 (N1,258.34).
Dangote gantry price raised to N874/litre from N774. MRS/NNPC pump price followed to ~N975.
Dangote gantry price surged to N1,275/litre. NNPC Lagos pump price N1,330, Abuja N1,361. Global crude oil price surge.
data.note: NNPC terminated exclusive purchase agreement with Dangote Refinery.
